end Solid

-- The following instances are not actually needed for the proof, but are included as part of the skeleton.
instance : HasLimitsOfSize.{0, 0} Solid := by
  have h₁ : HasLimitsOfSize.{0, 0} (SubsingletonType (Solid)) := by
    infer_instance
  have h₂ : HasLimitsOfSize.{0, 0} Solid := by
    have h₃ : ∀ {S : Sort*} [Subsingleton S], HasLimitsOfSize.{0, 0} (SubsingletonType S) := by
      intro S _S
      infer_instance
    -- We use the fact that `Solid` is a type with at most one element (a subsingleton) to infer that it has limits of all sizes.
    have h₄ : Subsingleton Solid := by
      -- Prove that `Solid` is a subsingleton.
      refine' ⟨fun x y => _⟩
      -- Use the fact that `x.toLightCondAb ≅ y.toLightCondAb` and the uniqueness of isomorphisms.
      have h₅ : x.toLightCondAb ≅ y.toLightCondAb := by
        -- Since both are of type `IsSolid`, we can use the uniqueness of the isomorphism.
        have h₆ : x.toLightCondAb = y.toLightCondAb := by
          -- Use the fact that the types are the same.
          have h₇ : x.toLightCondAb ≅ y.toLightCondAb := by
            -- Since both are of type `IsSolid`, we can use the uniqueness of the isomorphism.
            exact InvIso.refl (x.toLightCondAb)
          -- Use the uniqueness of the isomorphism.
          simp_all [IsSolid]
        -- Use the fact that the types are the same.
        exact Isomorphism.refl (x.toLightCondAb)
      -- Use the uniqueness of the isomorphism.
      have h₈ : x = y := by
        cases x
        cases y
        <;> simp_all [IsSolid]
        <;> aesop
      simp_all
    -- Since `Solid` is a subsingleton, it has limits of all sizes.
    exact h₃
  exact h₂
